ab: Manga’ay masaso’araw ho kita to anocila a helek no lahok han?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: Manga’ay to, icowa kita malalitemoh?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: Manga’ay i pitakcian kita haw? wa mamilu-ing kita.
ch: W:我們約學校好嗎?我們要錄影。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: I sefi sa mamanay, wa mamica’edong to losid a tayra kako.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿族服去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: Maka:pah ko kafocerak no congiliw no sakola no niyaro’ niyam, kafocerakan no congiliw tayni sato a mikilim i takowanan.
ch: W: 我們部落的櫻花盛開很漂亮。等花開的時候你來找我吧!
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: Anohatira tahira ho i kacanglahan, halafin tato!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，太晚了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: Mahaen i, pikiloma’an sato ko katayni iso.
ch: W: 那你豐年祭的時候來吧!
en: Then come during our Harvest Festival!

ab: Hayda, hinatala han to ako.
ch: M: 好啊!我很期待!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: Milicay ho tisowanan, hano patatikol a malinah tayni i niyaro’ kiso?
ch: W: 請問你為甚麼要搬回部落生活?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: Wa mamalasingsi ko kapinokay no mako.
ch: M: 我要回去教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: Hano caka i tokay a malasingsi?
ch: W: 為甚麼不在都市教書?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: Samanen ma’ilol kako to niyaro’ ako, nanay ira ko sapipacoay to riyaro’an.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: Tadamafana’ kiso a mitenooy, o cima to ko pasifana’ay i kisowanan?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: Ci fofo ako, i niyaro’ niyam cingra ko mafana’ay a mitenooy.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，她是我們部落最會織布的人。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: Mana sapinanamaw kiso a mitenooy?
ch: M:你怎麼會想要學織布?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: O mitenooyay ko serangawan ita, mangalay midotoc to serangawan kako.
ch: W:織布是我們的文化，我想要讓文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: Nga’ayay a kaenen koni a ^epah.
ch: M:這個小米酒真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: O niyah a misa^epahan yo!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: Idahi:ay to kiso, halafinay ko pisa^epah haw?
ch: M:你真厲害，這要釀很久嗎?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: Caka halafin, todong cecay a lipay nga’ayay to, i’ayaw rahkaden ho ko ko^tengay a malahakhak, nga, pacamolen to tamod samadahen a mitiyil, maedeng to paro han to i koreng.
ch: W:不用，大約一周就好了。把糯米先蒸熟，再加入酒麴一起拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. After steaming the sticky rice and white rice, we mix it with yeast powder, and then it can be stored in a jar.
